behavior.business_purpose: Onboard any git repo as a board project and reverse-engineer enough of its tech stack to drive it hands-off: create the project row + canonical statuses + non-null default branch + default skill, then derive every per-stack script the agent harness needs (install/setup, the merge-gate verify command, a boot-and-respond smoke check, edit-time feedback rules, a runnable green test scaffold, and clean-build/.gitignore hardening). This is the foundation that lets the board drive Node, Rust, Go, Python and JVM (Gradle/Kotlin/KMP/Spring/Ktor) repos, not just its own monorepo.

actors.name: operator
actors.notes: single local user registering a repo via UI 'Add Project' modal, CLI `pnpm cli -- register`, or MCP

actors.name: startup
actors.notes: boot-time deduplicateProjects() runs on every server start

actors.name: monitor
actors.notes: consumes the derived setup_script/verify_script downstream; only auto-starts a fully-driveable project

actors.name: review-merge
actors.notes: pre-merge gate runs the derived verify_script + smoke + opt-in cold-clone check

entry_points.observable: creates project (201 with {id,name,repoPath}); idempotent on same git root; fires best-effort profile/script derivation

entry_points.ref: Add Project modal (Import existing / Create new tabs)
entry_points.observable: register path or create+scaffold a new repo; errors render inline, modal stays open
registers a repo as a project
onboard or backfill driveable state + profile/scripts
merges duplicate registrations sharing one git root at boot

behaviors.statement: Registering a git repo creates a driveable project: a project row, canonical statuses, a default branch and default skill, returned synchronously as 201
preconditions: path is a valid git repo
behaviors.observable_outcome: POST /api/projects returns 201 with {id, name, repoPath}; project appears in GET /api/projects

behaviors.statement: Re-registering the same repo (or a subdirectory resolving to the same git root) returns the existing project instead of creating a duplicate
preconditions: a project already registered for that git root
behaviors.observable_outcome: result.created === false and the same project id is returned; no second row; issues/skills stay on one project

behaviors.statement: Registering with no repoPath (or a non-git path) is rejected with a 4xx and an explanatory error, leaving no project created
behaviors.observable_outcome: POST /api/projects returns 400 with error mentioning repoPath; duplicate path returns 409

behaviors.statement: A newly registered project never gets a null defaultBranch — it falls back to the repo's actually checked-out branch so the project is not silently undriveable (#772)
preconditions: repo's default branch is not named main/master, or detection returned null
behaviors.observable_outcome: project.defaultBranch is non-null when a branch is checked out; POST /api/workspaces does not 400 'No default branch configured'

behaviors.statement: Every new project is seeded the canonical 7-status set (incl. Backlog at -1) so issue batch-create and Backlog-pull auto-start work
behaviors.observable_outcome: GET /api/projects/:id/statuses lists Todo/In Progress/In Review/Done/Cancelled (+Backlog); POST /api/issues/batch does not 400 'No statuses found'

behaviors.statement: On startup, duplicate registrations resolving to one git root are merged into a single survivor — issues/skills/repos/scheduled-runs and the active-project pointer are moved, statuses remapped by name, duplicates deleted
preconditions: two project rows share one resolved git root (legacy subdir registration)
behaviors.observable_outcome: after boot only one project remains for that root; its issues/skills are intact; activeProjectId points at the survivor

behaviors.statement: repairProjectRegistration idempotently backfills driveable state onto an old/partial project — seeds statuses if none, sets defaultBranch if null, and populates profile/verify/setup scripts if unset — never clobbering existing values
preconditions: a project registered before profiles/scripts existed, or with missing statuses/branch
behaviors.observable_outcome: returns {seededStatuses, setDefaultBranch, populatedStackProfile, populatedVerifyScript, populatedSetupScript}; re-running is a no-op once filled

behaviors.statement: Marker files are reverse-engineered into one durable StackProfile across many stacks (node/pnpm/npm/yarn, rust/cargo, go, python/pip/poetry), inferring package manager, monorepo-ness, build/test/lint/dev commands, web-ness and dev port
behaviors.observable_outcome: detectStackProfile returns a typed profile with the correct stack/commands; empty repo yields a valid sparse profile (stack null, source 'detected')

behaviors.statement: The JVM/Gradle branch picks platform-correct, project-shape-correct commands: Kotlin vs Java compile task, KMP allTests vs test, Spring bootRun vs application run vs Ktor, .\gradlew.bat vs ./gradlew, dev-port scan, web-ness
behaviors.observable_outcome: profile reflects e.g. KMP allTests + no compileJava/dev; Ktor isWeb + run + port 8080/literal; Spring bootRun + isWeb; Java compileJava typecheck

behaviors.statement: The keystone merge gate is derived as `testCommand && buildCommand` (test first), persisted to verify_script_<projectId>, no-op on nothing-derivable, never clobbering a user/AI override
behaviors.observable_outcome: verify_script pref = 'pnpm test && pnpm build' (or test-only, or marker fallback); empty profile writes nothing; existing override survives

behaviors.statement: The monorepo-aware install/setup command is derived (pnpm install -r, gradle assemble, cargo fetch, …), persisted to projects.setup_script, no-op when empty and never clobbering an existing value
preconditions: a profile with installCommand or detectable markers
behaviors.observable_outcome: projects.setup_script = monorepo-aware install; single-package gets non-recursive install; empty detection leaves it null; existing value preserved

behaviors.statement: A boot-and-respond smoke check is built only for a bootable web/service project with a resolvable health URL — HTTP 200 plus HTML-shell needles for browser UIs, 200-only for headless APIs, null (skip) for CLI/library/no-URL
behaviors.observable_outcome: buildSmokeCheck returns {devCommand, healthUrl, expectBodyContains} for web UIs; [] needles for JSON APIs; null for non-web/no-URL

behaviors.statement: Edit-time feedback rules are generated cheapest-signal-first and scoped to the stack's source globs, with the slow-JVM family downgraded (no per-edit test rule, non-blocking typecheck)
behaviors.observable_outcome: .claude/smart-hooks-rules.json holds Typecheck/Quick-tests rules with stack-correct filePatterns; gradle gets no blocking per-edit test rule; no usable command → no rules

behaviors.statement: A trivially-passing runnable test is generated in the project's REAL test dir + runner syntax (vitest/pytest/cargo/go/JUnit/kotlin.test), and is never written into a dir that already has tests
behaviors.observable_outcome: writeTestScaffold writes one green test at the right path/extension; unknown stack writes nothing; existing tests are not clobbered/duplicated

behaviors.statement: New-project scaffold keeps main clean and clean-clones buildable: per-stack build-artifact + agent .gitignore (idempotent, no-clobber) and per-package-manager clean-build hardening (pnpm onlyBuiltDependencies, bun trustedDependencies, packageManager pin) so a fresh clone builds with no approval prompts
behaviors.observable_outcome: .gitignore gains the stack's artifact lines + agent lines once; package.json/pnpm config approves native postinstall + pins the manager; non-Node stacks are a clean no-op

behaviors.statement: The opt-in cold-clone build gate clones the committed branch into a fresh temp dir and runs install then build, failing the review on breakage that only appears on a truly clean clone, and always cleaning up the temp dir
preconditions: cold_clone_check_<projectId> enabled; a derivable build command
behaviors.observable_outcome: result.ok=false with reason build-failed/install-failed/clone-failed and failedCommand on breakage; ok=true 'no-build-command' when nothing derivable; temp clone always cleaned

behaviors.statement: When the rule-detected profile is sparse (no stack, or no test AND no build command) an LLM gap-fill is invoked and merged in ONLY where rules left null — rule facts win — and the whole derivation is fire-and-forget so it never slows or fails registration
behaviors.observable_outcome: persisted profile gains LLM-supplied commands only in null slots, source flips to 'llm'; registration returns immediately regardless of LLM outcome

permissions.notes: single-user local app — no RBAC; permission dimension is N/A (one actor)

error_states.statement: Registering a non-git path returns 4xx with 'not a git repository'
Re-registering an already-registered path returns 409 'already registered' (UI surfaces it inline)
cold-clone gate returns ok=false reason clone-failed/install-failed/build-failed
A hallucinated/hostile testCommand from a sparse-profile LLM gap-fill becomes the shell-executed verify gate with no allow-list — exploitation needs both a sparse-enough profile and a hostile command

external_integrations: claude-cli LLM (sparse-profile gap-fill + legacy generate* prompts)
filesystem (scaffold + smart-hooks + test-scaffold writes into the repo)

unknowns.question: Is the never-null defaultBranch guarantee (#772) asserted by any test, or only relied on in prod?
unknowns.why_unknown: no test imports registerProject/resolveDefaultBranch; e2e only asserts 201+id, not branch non-null

unknowns.question: Is deduplicateProjects ever exercised, not just mocked out?
unknowns.why_unknown: every test that touches it does vi.mock(...) to a no-op; no behavioral test found

unknowns.question: Does the LLM gap-fill merge rule (rules win, LLM fills only nulls) hold under a real/adversarial LLM JSON payload?
unknowns.why_unknown: enrichWithLlm/parseLlmJson have no direct test; the shell-exec trust boundary is untested

unknowns.question: Is profile staleness ever auto-invalidated when a repo changes stack?
unknowns.why_unknown: domain doc flags it inferred/unverified; only ?refresh=true recompute observed

gap.rationale: e2e asserts 201+{id,name,repoPath} but not the registration consequences as a journey (statuses seeded, branch non-null, skill attached) on the freshly-created project; UI test mocks the API so it only proves the modal flow

covered_by.test: packages/e2e/tests/ui/register-project.test.ts::duplicate path shows error message

gap.rationale: the UI test fulfills a MOCK 409; no server-level test proves registering the same git root (or a subdirectory of it) returns the existing project with created=false and no second row

packages/e2e/tests/api/projects.test.ts::GET /api/projects/:id/statuses returns statuses

gap.rationale: statuses are asserted on the long-lived E2E fixture project, not as a freshly-registered project's seeding consequence; the Backlog(-1) lane specifically is not asserted to exist

top_gaps.rationale: Server-level idempotency (same git root / legacy subdir → existing project, created=false, no duplicate) is only 'covered' by a mocked-409 UI test. Register the same repo twice (and a subdir of it) and assert one project id, created=false.
